motion: Defence asks to show the defendant's good character.
Prosecution wants to introduce hearsay from a stranger.
Defence objects: witness was never disclosed before trial.
Prosecution seeks to show a ten-year-old parking ticket.
Defence wants the jury to visit the crime scene at midnight.
Prosecution offers a properly authenticated document.
Defence objects because the officer read the witness's diary aloud.
Prosecution wants to play a video with no chain of custody.
Defence asks to mention the alleged victim's unrelated lawsuit.
Prosecution calls an expert with recognised qualifications.
Defence objects to a leading question on direct examination.
Prosecution wants to introduce a confession obtained without caution.
Defence asks to show the jury a cute dog photo unrelated to the case.
Prosecution offers DNA from a verified lab.
Defence objects: prosecutor is making arguments during questioning.
Prosecution wants to mention the defendant's previous acquittal.
reason: Prior acquittal cannot be used against them.
